results.title: Senior Data Scientist – Global Veterinary Pharmacovigilance, Signal Management | Jobflarely
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist – Global Veterinary Pharmacovigilance, Signal Management | Jobflarely
...
# Senior Data Scientist – Global Veterinary Pharmacovigilance, Signal Management
...
- Job Description:
- Support global pharmacovigilance signal management
- Advance signal detection and early alerting by leveraging new technologies
- Standardize signal review outputs and ensure consistent governance across diverse data sources
- Maintain end-to-end signal tracking and lifecycle documentation
- Continuously improve PV data quality and deliver “easy-to-digest” views
- Enable leadership-ready insight delivery through regular updates and ad hoc escalations
...
- Partner with PV leadership and digital/automation teams to evolve PV technology and signal management capabilities
...
- Requirements:
...
- MS or PhD in Data Science, Biostatistics, Epidemiology, Computer Science, or a related quantitative field
- 7+ years of experience with applied data analytics and handling and interpretation of multimodal data
- Senior‑level experience delivering analytics in a quality‑controlled or regulated environment (health, life sciences, or comparable)
- Strong technical skills in Python or R, SQL, and Git
- Relational database expertise
- Statistical modeling (e.g., linear modeling, ANOVA, Design of Experiments) and machine learning (supervised and unsupervised methods)
- Deep learning experience is a plus
- GenAI / Large Language Models (LLMs) — strongly preferred
- Familiarity with pharmacovigilance concepts and signal management workflows

results.title: Principal Data Scientist – R&D DSDH - Preclinical Sciences & Translational Safety (PSTS) | Hirevector
highlights:
Principal Data Scientist – R&D DSDH - Preclinical Sciences & Translational Safety (PSTS) | Hirevector
...
# Principal Data Scientist – R&D DSDH - Preclinical Sciences & Translational Safety (PSTS)
...
- Johnson & Johnson Innovative Medicine is recruiting for Principal Data Scientist – R&D DSDH - Preclinical Sciences & Translational Safety (PSTS). The primary location for this position is open to Spring House, PA; Titusville, NJ; Cambridge, MA; San Diego, CA; Beerse, Belgium; Madrid, Spain; or Barcelona, Spain.
...
- The R&D Data Science organization is seeking a Data Scientist to leverage advanced machine learning, robust data engineering techniques, and domain expertise to drive impactful decisions and generate actionable insights within the Pharmaceutical Sciences & Translational Safety (PSTS) organization. In this role, you will work closely with multidisciplinary teams—including toxicologists, PK/PD specialists, in vivo researchers, and safety professionals—to create AI‑ready datasets, develop predictive models, and deliver analytical solutions that promote improved safety evaluations and facilitate translational research.
...
- Key Responsibilities Develop and deploy ML/AI models to support safety signal detection, dose selection, PK/PD modeling, toxicology insights, and translational interpretation. Implement representation‑learning, predictive modeling, and multivariate analytics for datasets spanning in vivo studies, in vitro assays, exposure‑response data, and pathology information. Partner with scientific SMEs to design modeling strategies aligned with PSTS decision points. Apply model governance, versioning, and validation standards consistent with R&D AI practices. Build and maintain scalable data pipelines that integrate PSTS‑relevant data sources. Transform raw experimental outputs into standardized, analysis‑ready, AI‑ready datasets using Python, R, and cloud‑native services. Contribute to harmonized scientific data models in collaboration with data engineering and ontology teams. Work directly with toxicology, DMPK, and safety stakeholders to interpret scientific context and translate study designs into computational requirements. Apply understanding of mechanism‑based toxicology, exposure‑response concepts, and in vivo study structures to guide data transformations and modeling strategies. Enhance cross‑study comparability via standardized terminologies, metadata practices, and quality checks. Collaborate with PSTS functional experts, R&D Data Science teams, and platform architects to ensure high‑quality, scalable data solutions. Qualifications
...
- Advanced degree (MS or PhD) in Data Science, Computational Biology, Toxicology, Pharmacology, Biomedical Engineering, Computer Science, or related field. 3+ years of experience applying machine learning and/or data engineering to scientific or biomedical datasets. Proficiency with Python and/or R, SQL, and modern data engineering tooling (cloud computing, workflow orchestration, version control). Experience with ML model development, evaluation, and deployment pipelines. Experience working with biological, toxicology, PK/PD, or in vivo datasets.
...
- Experience in safety sciences, ADME/DMPK, toxicogenomics, or biomarker analytics. Familiarity with scientific data formats (e.g., assay outputs, histopathology data, PK time‑course datasets). Exposure to ontologies, semantic technologies, or knowledge graph integration for scientific domains. Experience with cloud‑based data architectures. Understanding of regulatory data standards (e.g., SEND, CDISC). Equal Opportunity Employer

results.title: Zoetis AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Zoetis, the world's leading animal health company, has established itself as a pioneer in AI-powered veterinary diagnostics and data-driven innovation [1]. The company's flagship AI product, Vetscan Imagyst, represents the first and only veterinary AI analyzer offering seven distinct testing capabilities including AI Fecal, AI Urine Sediment, AI Dermatology, AI Blood Smear, AI Equine Fecal Egg Count, AI Masses, and Digital Cytology [2]. Beyond diagnostics, Zoetis is leveraging AI across its entire biopharma value chain, from drug discovery and clinical development to manufacturing and commercial operations [3]. With over $650 million invested in R&D annually and a comprehensive digital transformation strategy, the company is positioning itself to lead the next era of animal health innovation in a market projected to reach $90 billion by 2035 [4].

results.title: PK/PD Senior Scientist | Jobflarely
highlights:
PK/PD Senior Scientist | Jobflarely
...
# PK/PD Senior Scientist
...
2026
...
Zoetis, a global leader in animal health, seeks an enthusiastic colleague to join our Veterinary Medicine Research and Development (VMRD) PK/PD Team to discover and develop novel therapeutics for veterinary medicine. The successful candidate will be primarily responsible for designing, conducting, analyzing, and interpreting in vivo PK/PD studies in multiple species in support of projects from early research through late-stage development. This position will require a highly motivated and independent individual who can effectively collaborate with other team members to advance projects.
...
### POSITION RESPONSIBILITIES:
...
- Design and analyze in vivo PK/PD, PK and TK studies in multiple veterinary species, including writing study protocols and reports.
...
- Provide PK and PK/PD modeling and simulation to help select lead candidates and optimize dose regimens.
...
- Collaborate with bioanalytical team to assess suitability of analytical assays and forecast analytical requirements for projects.
...
- Lead development of scientific strategy across therapeutic areas with a focus on mechanistic platforms/biomarkers to aid in translation, measurement of target engagement, and dose regimen predictions.
...
- Work collaboratively and communicate effectively with multidisciplinary teams to design and prosecute studies in line with the strategic direction of the project.
...
- Present research results to scientific teams, management, and regulatory agencies.
...
- Write sections of regulatory submissions, respond to inquiries from regulatory agencies, and maintain knowledge of regulatory guidance documents.
...
REQUIRED EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE:
...
- Ph.D in pharmacology, pharmaceutical sciences, biochemistry, or related field. 2-4 years of post-doctoral experience in pharmaceutical development is highly desirable. DVM DACVCP or DVM Dipl. ECVPT is highly desirable.
...
- Proven ability to design, analyze and interpret in vivo PK and PK/PD studies. Experience with biotherapeutics (protein therapeutics) is highly desirable.
...
- Skilled in the use of PK and PK/PD analysis software such as WinNonlin, Monolix or Watson.
...
- Must have excellent verbal & written communication skills.
...
- Self-starter, able to prioritize work and work efficiently with minimal supervision.
...
- Working knowledge of bioanalytical methodology.
- Experience in PK/PD modeling and simulation such as PBPK, QSP or population PK modeling using NONMEM, R, or related software is highly desirable.

results.title: Zoetis Data Scientist Interview Questions & Guide 2026
highlights:
### What is a Data Scientist at Zoetis?
...
A Data Scientist at Zoetis plays a pivotal role in transforming the landscape of animal health. As the world’s leading animal health company, Zoetis leverages data science to discover, develop, manufacture, and commercialize medicines, vaccines, and diagnostic products for livestock and companion animals. In this role, you are not just building models; you are directly contributing to solutions that predict disease outbreaks, optimize therapeutic pipelines, and enhance the well-being of animals globally.
...
The impact of this position spans across multiple critical business units. You will collaborate with research and development (R&D) teams to analyze genomic and clinical trial data, work with supply chain leaders to optimize manufacturing yields, and partner with commercial teams to deliver precision veterinary medicine solutions. By translating complex biological and business datasets into actionable predictive models, you help veterinarians and livestock producers make faster, more informed decisions.
...
This role is ideal for professionals who thrive at the intersection of advanced analytics and tangible, real-world applications. Whether you are developing computer vision models for livestock monitoring or utilizing natural language processing to analyze veterinary records, your work at Zoetis has a direct, measurable footprint on global food security and companion animal care.

understanding of statistical modeling, machine learning theory

and trade-

...
### Applied Machine Learning & Technical Fundamentals
...
This area focuses on your core machine learning knowledge. Unlike software-heavy tech companies, Zoetis rarely fields abstract Leetcode-style algorithmic questions; instead, they focus on practical machine learning theory and application.

and precision-recall (or bias vs. recall) tradeoffs.

hand off models to data engineers for production deployment.

...

...
- Nice-to-have skills – Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP), familiarity with big data technologies (Spark, Databricks), and prior exposure to life sciences, bioinformatics, or clinical data.
...
Q: How technical are the interviews at Zoetis? A: The interviews are highly technical but focus heavily on applied machine learning and practical problem-solving rather than abstract software engineering puzzles. You should expect questions about model trade-offs, statistics, and business case studies, but you will not face standard Leetcode coding challenges.
...
Securing a Data Scientist role at Zoetis is an opportunity to apply advanced analytics to a highly meaningful and impactful domain. The interview process is thorough but fair, prioritizing practical machine learning applications, structured problem-solving, and strong communication skills over abstract coding puzzles.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

results.title: LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
highlights:
LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
...
# LLM Engineer
...
## Job Details
...
VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact.
...
The VIB Center for Neuroscience Leuven (CNL) investigates the mechanisms of brain function and neurological disease across the scales of biology, spanning neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment, synaptic biology, and computational neuroscience. AI and large language models have transformative potential across these research areas.
...
We are now looking for an LLM Engineer in a shared position between VIB.AI's Machine Learning Expertise Unit and the Center for Neuroscience. You will split your time between building institute-wide AI tools and applying them directly to neuroscience research challenges.
...
As an LLM Engineer in this shared position, you will:
...
- Develop and deploy LLM-based pilot projects tailored to neuroscience research at the CNL, applying AI to questions in molecular, cellular and circuit neuroscience, neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment and disease mechanisms.
...
- Build and maintain components of VIB's institutional intelligence layer, including agentic bioinformatics workflows, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, and AI-assisted experimental design tools.
...
- Design, implement, and evaluate LLM-based solutions for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.
...
- Collaborate with wet-lab and computational neuroscience researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI and LLMs can accelerate scientific progress.
...
- Work with the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and VIB's AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools that serve the broader VIB community.
...
- Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of LLMs in line with best practices and international standards.
...
- Mine opportunities and data at the center’s advanced technology expertise centers and affiliated central core facilities.
...
- Leverage VIB's Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale LLM development and deployment.
...
- PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, computational neuroscience, statistics, or a related field.
- Strong experience with large language models, including pretraining, fine-tuning, prompt engineering, and evaluation.
- Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).
- Experience with real-world applications of LLMs in scientific or biomedical contexts is a plus.
...
- Familiarity with neuroscience data types (imaging, electrophysiology, genomics, proteomics, or clinical data) is a plus but not required.
...
- Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.
- A strong interest in applying LLMs to impactful life science and neuroscience problems.
...
- The opportunity to pioneer applications of LLMs in neuroscience and the broader life sciences.
...
- A unique position at the interface of an AI platform team and a world-class neuroscience research center, with exposure to both engineering and scientific challenges.
...
- Direct access to VIB's Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.
...

...
For further information, please send an email to stein.aerts@kuleuven.be or joost.schymkowitz@kuleuven.be with subject title Inquiry: LLM Engineer CNL/VIB.AI.

Optum, part of UnitedHealth Group, is a global health services and technology company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through data analytics, pharmacy benefits, and digital health solutions that connect millions to better care. Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.
